core_loop: Named NPCs hold Conviction(s)+Resonant Style+Certainty (npc_behavior_v30 §1-§2); players target vulnerabilities in Contest via RS-matching (§6), NPCs accrue per-Conviction Scars from witnessed Thread ops (conviction_track_v1 §3) or Contest losses, Scars gate Belief revision and fire authored per-NPC Arc branches (§5.2). BG-mode Priority Trees (§8) run deterministic 7-level faction decisions each season; Outreach/Demand (§8.11) surfaces ≤3 NPC-initiated scenes/season to the player.

decision_points.options: Evidence/Consequence/Authority/Solidarity, locked before Exchange 1, no mid-Contest pivot
decision_points.note: tactically real but collapses to lookup once NPC's fixed RS is known (§1.3 roster)

decision_points.where: Advisor Knot extraction vs public citation (§3.5.3-3.5.4)
decision_points.options: extract privately (no cost) vs cite publicly (full rupture)
one of 5 fixed incentive types, max one, stacking = coercion
binary accept/refuse per entry, capped 3/2 per season
name a specific verifiable future condition
decision_points.note: only one NPC has this open-ended authored mechanism

decision_points.where: Which authored Arc endpoint an NPC resolves to (§5.2 Arc Maps)
decision_points.options: fixed small menu per NPC (Almud A-F, Himlensendt A-C, etc.), state-gated timing

north_star.collision_feed: Feeds Conviction/Scar/Disposition/priority-tree-fired state into Contest targeting, faction Cascade, and Outreach — genuine collision-engine substrate (T-23/T-24 chains per PP-718 vetting) — but feed quality is degraded by an unresolved 2-cycle damper dispute and a doc:null memory sink.
north_star.verdict: Points at the North Star in structure (state-driven branching, strong dramatic legibility) but under-delivers on 'myriad options': RS choice collapses to lookup, faction AI is fully predictable, and arc destinations are authored menus rather than emergent trajectories.

nq.observation: BG Priority Trees (§8.2-§8.10) are deterministic if-then ladders per faction with no stochastic branching beyond embedded rolls — a read player can predict faction action every season, in tension with the non-dominance clause.
Dramatic-legibility test passes well here: Stance Triangles give explicit Beliefs (who/what), Generic Transition Triggers + Priority Trees give deterministic no-action consequences.

Core rule is not restatable after one reading: Conviction taxonomy + per-Conviction Scar + Certainty + Resonant Style + 7-level Priority Tree + Outreach/Demand + Arc trees stack into a large apparatus.
PP-718 vetting grounds per-Conviction Scar in a specific Renaissance analogy (Catherine de' Medici multi-frame moral injury) — a strong, non-generic N argument.

threadwork_junctures.juncture: Knot strain propagation from transforming NPC
threadwork_junctures.evidence: npc_behavior_v30.md §5.0b, explicit P-12 fix, Close/Distant strain by TS band

threadwork_junctures.juncture: Faction Priority Trees gated on Thread state
threadwork_junctures.evidence: Only Varfell §8.5 (rows 2/2b) and Wardens §8.10 (rows 1-3) have Thread-conditioned rows; Church/Crown/Hafenmark/Guilds/Löwenritter/RM trees (§8.2-§8.4,§8.6,§8.7,§8.9) have zero, despite P-01 inseparability implying Thread events in their territories should shape institutional posture

threadwork_junctures.evidence: module_contracts.yaml npc_memory block: doc:null, schema only inside political_dynamics_keys_migration_v30 §2.3, no standalone P-09 mechanical surface

legibility.note: Stance Triangles + Beliefs (§2.x) answer whose-position/what-they-want directly; §5.1 Generic Transition Triggers and per-faction Priority Trees give deterministic next-season-if-no-action answers (e.g. Stability<=1 collapses to Autonomy; Almud's Arc A window closes permanently at Counter=2 while Certainty>=2, §5.2).

degenerate_candidates: Fully deterministic BG Priority Trees (§8.1-§8.10) — no randomization beyond embedded rolls, predictable per faction every season
Fixed 1:1 Resonant Style per NPC (§1.3 + roster) + stacking Wrong-Style Penalty (§6.4.3) collapses contest tactics to lookup-and-apply once NPC's RS is known

edges.cite: mechanics_index.yaml line 305 cites wrong file vs module_contracts.yaml piety_track block

directions.top-down: §12 Canon Compliance table only rows P-01/02/06/12 and mostly reads 'not directly invoked' rather than demonstrating active compliance against the other 11 gates.
directions.bottom-up: sim/personal/conviction.py's CONVICTIONS constant still hardcodes the legacy 9-Conviction set the file's own comment calls superseded, not PP-684/718's ratified 13-set — the Godot-bound reference has not caught up to the passed vetting.
directions.lateral: Only 2 of 9 faction Priority Trees (Varfell, Wardens) have any Thread-conditioned row; siblings (Church, Crown, Hafenmark, Guilds, Löwenritter, RM) have none.
directions.diagonal: Per-Conviction Scar erosion is declared to propagate to faction-scale Cascade math (PP-686 §3.2) per the PP-718 vetting doc, but no module_contracts edge confirms this is implemented.
directions.forwards: No workplan lane entry or HANDOFF reference found scheduling npc_behavior/conviction_track work next; this lane appears currently unowned in active continuity tracking.
directions.backwards: Two same-stem-named docs (designs/scene/conviction_track_v30.md vs designs/personal/conviction_track_v1.md) cover unrelated mechanics; canon/mechanics_index.yaml fell into exactly this citation trap while module_contracts.yaml did not.

findings.claim: module_contracts.yaml's own two blocks for the identical social_contest<->npc_behavior 2-cycle disagree: social_contest's block asserts BOUNDED with a verification ref and explicit numeric bounds, while npc_behavior's block for the same loop asserts dampers unconfirmed/OPEN.
findings.tracked_ref: 02_interdependency_map.md line 25 flags 'dampers unconfirmed [OPEN — Jordan]'; this finding adds the specific self-contradiction (one block claims resolved-and-bounded) as new severity evidence

findings.claim: sim/personal/conviction.py's CONVICTIONS constant still implements the legacy 9-Conviction taxonomy rather than PP-684/PP-718's ratified 13-Conviction structured-concentration set, so the Godot-validating reference has not been migrated despite the design-level vetting passing.

canon/mechanics_index.yaml's conviction_scar entry cites designs/scene/conviction_track_v30.md#section-1 (the unrelated per-territory Piety-stat BG mechanic) instead of designs/personal/conviction_track_v1.md, contradicting module_contracts.yaml's correct piety_track entry and npc_behavior_v30's own explicit redirect.

npc_memory is doc:null with only an [ASSUMPTION]-grade state_reader resolver and a schema buried in a different system's subsection, so P-09 (memory pulling must be messy/costly/detectable) has no discoverable mechanical surface for named-NPC memory despite npc_behavior emitting directly into it.
findings.tracked_ref: CLAUDE.md §6 / ED-1051 doc:null backlog (generic); P-09-specific angle is added scope

findings.claim: All 9 faction Priority Trees are fixed deterministic if-then ladders with no stochastic branching beyond embedded contest rolls, letting a player who has read the corpus predict faction action every season — tension with Omega non-dominance.

Each named NPC has exactly one fixed Primary (and usually Secondary) Resonant Style, and the stacking Wrong-Style Penalty (§6.4.3) punishes any other choice, so contest tactics collapse from a 4-style system into 'look up the NPC's answer key.'
The §12 Canon Compliance table addresses only 4 of 15 P-numbers and mostly with 'not directly invoked' rather than demonstrated compliance, leaving P-03/04/05/08/10/11/13/15 unaddressed even as N/A.

Only Varfell and Wardens Priority Trees have Thread-state-conditioned rows; Church, Crown, Hafenmark, Guilds, Löwenritter, and RM trees have none, despite P-01 inseparability implying Thread events in their territories should shape institutional posture.
